concept: Encouragement / To Build a Bridge to the Heart's Path
description: The act of giving support, confidence, or hope to someone. It is the process of building a bridge to another's core to help them find their own strength and path.
rationale: This word extends its verbal sibling 'korea' (to encourage) with the flowing 'la', transforming a momentary act into sustained support. Where 'korea' is the hand extended, 'korela' is the ongoing presence that walks beside. The shared opening 'ko-re' maintains that bridge of strength, but 'la' carries it forward into continuous flow—encouragement not as a single gesture but as a river of support. The extra syllable gives weight to what has been created: not just a moment of uplift but a lasting current of belief that continues to flow even when the encourager is no longer present.
